summary: Iter11 backend testing of three concurrent-webhook / dedup fixes on 355 LLC. 9/9 new tests pass + 21/21 existing unit tests still pass. Verified: (1) unique partial index company_plaid_txn_uniq exists with correct spec and enforces E11000 on duplicate (company_id, plaid_transaction_id); (2) merchant_cache.upsert rejects low-conf (<0.85) LLM writes on both insert-new AND update-existing paths, while source='user' remains authoritative; (3) _sync_and_import processes synced['removed'] and deletes stale pending txns; (4) _categorize_and_insert survives BulkWriteError from concurrent-webhook duplicates via ordered=False + try/except — the non-duplicate txn Y still lands while duplicate X is skipped. Regression: 355 LLC has 1878 txns, balance sheet imbalance = $0.00, /api/auth/login, /api/companies, /api/companies/{cid}/accounts, /transactions, /reports/balance-sheet all 200 OK. No real Plaid calls; sync_transactions was monkeypatched.

minor.issue: server.py @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') uses deprecated FastAPI API — should migrate to lifespan handlers (warning only, still functional).

critical_code_review_comments: merchant_cache.upsert low-confidence guard uses < 0.85 as the threshold in TWO places (insert & update) — good, but a module-level constant MIN_LLM_CONFIDENCE = 0.85 would prevent drift.
server._sync_and_import iterates synced['removed'] with N delete_one() calls; could be a single delete_many({'plaid_transaction_id': {'$in': ids}}) for perf on large removed batches.
server.py is still 2174 lines — flagged in iter10 as well. Splitting into routers (auth/companies/transactions/plaid/reports) is overdue.
_categorize_and_insert logs 'insert_many partial' but does not distinguish dedup-drops from real errors; consider inspecting e.details['writeErrors'][*]['code'] and only swallowing code=11000.

conftest.py added a session-scoped event_loop fixture to work around motor's AsyncIOMotorClient being bound to the loop at import time. Cleaner: refactor db.py to lazily create the client per-loop or use a factory.
test_credentials: pro@axiom.ai / pro123 (login returns {token, user}; note: response key is `token`, not `access_token`)
seed_data_creation: All test data uses fresh UUID-prefixed company_ids (test-idx-*, test-mc-*, test-remove-*, test-dup-*) with try/finally cleanup — no touch to real 355 LLC data. Zero real Plaid API calls (monkeypatched plaid_service.sync_transactions).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter11 test file at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration11_dedup_fixes.py is stable and idempotent. It uses its own shared asyncio loop via helper `run()` because motor's client (created at db.py import) binds to whichever loop is current then; a session-scoped conftest.event_loop is also present. To run: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_iteration11_dedup_fixes.py -v` (pytest.ini's -n 2 loadscope handles parallelism safely). 355 LLC company_id = 5f744222-ac09-4724-b48a-733e09952c36. OBE account for 355 LLC is code 3050 (not 3900).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — all three fixes verified working. (a) Unique partial index blocks concurrent-race duplicates at the DB layer. (b) merchant_cache guards against low-confidence poisoning both on new-insert and update-over-existing branches. (c) Removed-array handling drops stale pending txns on pending→posted transition. (d) insert_many(ordered=False)+try/except lets partial batches land when some rows collide with the new unique index.
